Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with query "Wednesday" or "schedule" or "calendar" (or similar) to discover the overlapping meetings on Robert's Wednesday schedule. Expected findings: Three emails requesting meetings on Wednesday April 29 - (1) Grace Yamamoto's "Operational Readiness Review - Wednesday April 29, 9:00 AM" requesting 9:00-10:30 AM, (2) David Chen from UWM's "UWM Quarterly Business Review - Wednesday April 29, 9:30 AM (CONFIRMED)" for 9:30-11:00 AM which cannot be rescheduled without 2 weeks notice, and (3) Denise Holloway's "Confidential: HR Follow-up Required - Wednesday April 29" requesting 10:00-10:45 AM.
OE 1b: Search or list emails in the SENT folder using search_emails with query "Wednesday" and folder_name "SENT", or list_emails with folder_name "SENT" (or similar), to discover Priya's own summary email to Robert. Expected findings: Priya's email "Wednesday Prep - Three Critical Items Requiring Your Attention" (email_scenario_7x01) sent Monday evening (April 27) to Robert, summarizing all three issues: the E&O insurance gap, the Rachel Kim email forwarding data breach, and the three overlapping Wednesday meetings with their time slots and constraints.
OE 2: Read the full email from Grace Yamamoto using get_email_by_id with email_id "email_scenario_7c01" (or similar) to get the Operational Readiness Review agenda details. Expected findings: Grace's email to Robert (CC Priya) proposing 9:00-10:30 AM on Wednesday April 29 with agenda items including Q1 pipeline review, LOS system upgrade timeline (Raj joining), vendor contract renewals (Priya to present), insurance and compliance updates, and post-Marcus Webb process improvements.
OE 3: Read the UWM QBR confirmation email using get_email_by_id with email_id "email_scenario_7c02" (or similar). Expected findings: David Chen (david.chen@uwm.com) from United Wholesale Mortgage confirming the QBR for 9:30-11:00 AM on Wednesday April 29 via Zoom. The email states rescheduling requires 2 weeks advance notice per partnership agreement, making this meeting immovable. Agenda includes YTD volume review, rate environment outlook, Preferred Partner tier renewal, and new product rollout.
OE 4: Read Denise Holloway's confidential HR meeting request using get_email_by_id with email_id "email_scenario_7c03" (or similar). Expected findings: Denise requesting 10:00-10:45 AM on Wednesday April 29 to discuss security audit findings, NC Identity Theft Protection Act obligations, and federal regulations. Meeting must include only Robert, Denise, and Priya. She states it cannot wait and references regulatory deadlines.
OE 5: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Wednesday" or "schedule" or "readiness review" (or similar) to find internal discussions about the scheduling conflicts. Expected findings: Priya's message in #general (C001) warning that Robert has a packed Wednesday schedule, and DM conversations with Grace about rescheduling the Ops Readiness Review.
OE 6: Retrieve DM conversation history between Priya and Grace using conversations_history on channel D_grace_priya (or similar, discovered via channels_list with channel_types "im"). Expected findings: Grace's Friday message asking Priya to put the Ops Review on Robert's calendar and mentioning the insurance situation. Priya's reply noting the UWM QBR conflict at 9:30. On Tuesday (Apr 28), Priya asking Grace to move the Readiness Review to the afternoon or split it. Grace pushing back, saying Raj is only available in the morning for the LOS upgrade discussion, and proposing to keep 9:00-9:30 for the most critical items then reconvene in the afternoon.
OE 7: Retrieve DM conversation history between Priya and Denise using conversations_history on channel D_denise_priya (or similar). Expected findings: Denise's urgent message about needing the confidential meeting Wednesday at 10 AM regarding the email forwarding security audit finding, stating it cannot be rescheduled due to regulatory deadlines. Priya's reply acknowledging the urgency and explaining the Wednesday morning scheduling mess with UWM and Grace's meetings.
OE 8: Search emails using search_emails with query "insurance" or "E&O" (or similar) to discover the insurance coverage gap issue. Expected findings: Hana Kim's email "Missing E&O Insurance Payments - Urgent" reporting no E&O insurance payments after February 2026, South Park Insurance Group showing as inactive in QuickBooks, last bill was BILL-REC-8ea37fda2b5e for $702.86. Also discovers Sandra Mitchell's cold outreach from Pinnacle Professional Insurance offering E&O coverage starting at $685/month with 3-5 day binding.
OE 9: Search emails using search_emails with query "security audit" or "email forwarding" or "Rachel Kim" (or similar) to discover the data breach finding. Expected findings: Raj Anand's confidential email reporting that Rachel Kim (terminated 2025-06-30) has an active email forwarding rule sending all keystonemortgage.com emails to rachel.kim.personal@gmail.com. Approximately 340 emails forwarded over 10 months, potentially including borrower SSNs, loan applications, title company documents, and lender correspondence. Other terminated employees (Brian Mitchell, Todd Jennings, Andre Washington, Veronica Hayes) were properly deactivated.
OE 10: Read the full email thread for the security audit finding using get_email_by_id with email_id "email_scenario_7b03" (or similar) to get Raj's follow-up details. Expected findings: Raj estimates approximately 340 emails forwarded, server-side logs available showing emails from borrowers, title companies (including Tryon Title & Escrow), and at least two lenders. Rachel Kim's LOS access was properly revoked on termination date but the email forwarding was a server-side rule missed during offboarding. Raj has drafted an updated offboarding checklist.
OE 11: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "security audit" or "audit" (or similar) to find related Slack discussions. Expected findings: Raj's message in #it-support (C008) about completing the terminated employee account review and finding one issue needing management attention. Denise's message in #compliance-alerts (C004) requesting Priya find time on Robert's calendar for a confidential compliance matter related to security audit findings.
OE 12: Retrieve DM conversation between Priya and Raj using conversations_history on channel D_raj_priya (or similar). Expected findings: Raj's urgent DM alerting Priya to the security audit email about a former employee email forwarding issue that could be a data breach. Priya's reply saying she will set up an emergency meeting with Robert and Denise and instructing Raj not to touch anything.
OE 13: Retrieve DM conversation between Priya and Hana using conversations_history on channel D_hana_priya (or similar). Expected findings: Hana's DM emphasizing the E&O insurance gap - last payment to South Park Insurance was February, two months with potentially no coverage, vendor marked inactive in QuickBooks. Priya's reply that she may have received an email from a new insurance provider she had not opened yet.
OE 14: Search QuickBooks vendors using quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria to find South Park Insurance Group (or similar). Expected findings: South Park Insurance Group (V-SOUTHPARK-INS) with Active: false, confirming the vendor is inactive as reported by Hana.
OE 15: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to South Park Insurance or E&O insurance (or similar) to verify the last payment. Expected findings: The last E&O insurance bill is BILL-REC-8ea37fda2b5e for $702.86 dated Feb 2026. No bills exist for March or April 2026 from South Park Insurance Group, confirming a 2-month coverage gap.
OE 16: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements (or similar) to find notes about the insurance and security issues. Expected findings: Three relevant engagement notes - (1) "E&O Insurance Coverage Gap - Critical" noting the 2-month lapse and Pinnacle's $685/mo quote, (2) "Security Audit - Former Employee Email Forwarding Issue" detailing the Rachel Kim situation with approximately 340 forwarded emails, and (3) "Combined Risk: Insurance Gap + Data Breach Exposure" noting that the E&O coverage gap compounds the data breach liability since Keystone has no professional liability protection for any resulting claims.
OE 17: Look up Rachel Kim's staff record using mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false (or similar) to verify her employment status. Expected findings: Rachel Kim (los_staff_266153ea2738), role: processor, is_active: false, termination_date: 2025-06-30, confirming the details in Raj's email.
OE 18: Search for Sandra Mitchell in contacts using contacts_search_contacts with query "Sandra Mitchell" or "Pinnacle" (or similar) to get her contact information. Expected findings: Sandra Mitchell, Commercial Lines Agent at Pinnacle Professional Insurance, email sandra.mitchell@pinnacleprofins.com, phone (704) 555-0193.
OE 19: Search CRM companies using crm_search_companies with name "Pinnacle" or "UWM" or "United Wholesale" (or similar) to get company details. Expected findings: Pinnacle Professional Insurance (crm_co_pinnacle_ins01) as a vendor specializing in E&O coverage, and United Wholesale Mortgage (crm_co_uwm_lender01) as Keystone's largest wholesale lender and Preferred Partner.
OE 20: Create a comprehensive briefing document for Robert by saving it to the system using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", or filesystem_write_file, or similar. The briefing should cover all three critical items: (1) the E&O insurance coverage gap since February 2026 with South Park Insurance inactive and Pinnacle Professional Insurance as a replacement option at $685/month, (2) the Rachel Kim email forwarding data breach with approximately 340 emails forwarded to personal Gmail over 10 months including potential borrower PII, regulatory notification obligations under NC Identity Theft Protection Act, and (3) the compounding risk that the insurance gap leaves Keystone without E&O coverage during the data breach period. The note should include the people involved, the timeline, the external parties (Sandra Mitchell at Pinnacle, David Chen at UWM), and the decisions Robert needs to make.
OE 21: Send an email (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@keystonemortgage.com to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com proposing a concrete rescheduling plan for the Operational Readiness Review. The email should acknowledge Grace's constraint about Raj's morning availability, propose keeping a shortened 9:00-9:30 AM session for the most critical items (insurance and compliance updates) before the UWM QBR starts, and suggest reconvening in the afternoon for the remaining agenda items (pipeline review, LOS upgrade with Raj to be rescheduled separately, vendor contracts). The proposal must work around the immovable UWM QBR (9:30-11:00 AM) and Denise's confidential HR meeting (10:00-10:45 AM).
OE 22: Send an email (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@keystonemortgage.com to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com confirming the time for the confidential HR meeting on Wednesday April 29 at 10:00-10:45 AM with Robert. The email should reassure Denise that the time slot is confirmed and protected, mention that other meetings have been adjusted to accommodate it, and maintain confidentiality by not disclosing details of what the meeting is about in the email body.
OE 23: Optionally post a message in Slack (via conversations_add_message) to Denise's DM channel (D_denise_priya or similar) confirming the Wednesday meeting time is locked in, as an additional confirmation channel beyond the email in OE 22. The prompt says to "confirm the time with Denise so she stops worrying," which can be satisfied by email alone (OE 22) or by both email and Slack DM.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results

2 The briefing document (CRM engagement note) mentions the E&O insurance coverage gap, specifically that South Park Insurance Group is inactive and no premium payments have been made since February 2026, leaving Keystone potentially uninsured for approximately 2 months.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The briefing document explicitly mentions South Park Insurance Group as INACTIVE in QuickBooks, notes the last recorded E&O premium payment was in February 2026, and estimates an approximately 2-month coverage gap through late April.
✓Run #3The briefing document explicitly states 'Feb 2026: Last recorded E&O premium payment to South Park Insurance Group', 'South Park Insurance Group is marked INACTIVE in QuickBooks', and '~2 months with potentially lapsed E&O coverage'.
✓Run #4The briefing document explicitly mentions the E&O insurance gap, states 'no E&O insurance premium payments have been recorded after February 2026,' references South Park Insurance Group as INACTIVE, and notes 'approximately 2 months' of potential no coverage.
✓Run #5The briefing document explicitly states 'no E&O insurance premium payments have been made since February 2026,' that 'South Park Insurance Group appears to have gone inactive,' and that 'Keystone may have been operating WITHOUT Errors & Omissions coverage for approximately 2 months (March-April 2026).'
✓Run #6The briefing document explicitly covers the E&O insurance gap, names South Park Insurance Group as INACTIVE in QuickBooks, and notes the last bill was from February 2026 with no March/April payments, indicating approximately 2 months of potential uninsured exposure since March 1.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The briefing explicitly cites 'NC data breach notification law (NC G.S. § 75-65)' and 'Federal requirements (GLBA Safeguards Rule)' in the regulatory context section.
✓Run #2The briefing document explicitly references the NC Identity Theft Protection Act and federal requirements (GLBA Safeguards Rule) in Section 2B, noting Denise's compliance assessment.
✓Run #3The briefing document explicitly mentions 'NC Identity Theft Protection Act: notification requirements if personal information of NC residents was compromised' and 'Federal regulations (GLBA, etc.) may also apply'.
✓Run #4The briefing document explicitly references 'NC Identity Theft Protection Act, federal data breach notification requirements' as applicable regulations, directly addressing the regulatory notification obligations.
✓Run #5The briefing explicitly states 'NC Identity Theft Protection Act requires notification to affected individuals if personal information was accessed by unauthorized persons' and mentions 'Federal requirements may also apply (FinCEN SAR filing, depending on scope).'
✓Run #6The briefing document in Section 3 explicitly references the 'NC Identity Theft Protection Act' and the requirement to notify affected individuals 'without unreasonable delay' after discovery of a breach.
6 The briefing document mentions the compounding risk that the E&O insurance gap and the data breach are related, specifically that Keystone has no professional liability coverage during the period when borrower PII may have been compromised.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The briefing has an explicit 'CONNECTION TO THE E&O GAP' section stating: 'If the email forwarding incident results in borrower complaints or regulatory action, the normal first line of defense would be the E&O policy. With coverage potentially lapsed since March, Keystone would be uninsured for any claims arising from this breach.'
✓Run #2The briefing document has a 'CONNECTION TO E&O INSURANCE GAP' subsection explicitly stating: 'Keystone may face a data breach notification obligation...at the same time the firm may have no E&O insurance coverage,' and warns about uninsured liability exposure.
✓Run #3Section 4 of the briefing ('HOW THESE ISSUES INTERSECT') explicitly explains that if the breach triggers regulatory action or borrower claims, the normal protection would be E&O insurance which may not exist, and notes 'any claims arising after the policy lapsed (March 2026 onward) may be uncovered.'
✓Run #4The briefing document explicitly states under Issue #2: 'combined with the potential data breach in Issue #1, the exposure is compounded. If the Rachel Kim email forwarding results in a claim, borrower lawsuit, or regulatory action, Keystone would have no professional liability coverage to respond.'
✓Run #5The briefing's 'INTERCONNECTION OF ISSUES' section explicitly states 'The E&O gap means the company is financially exposed on any claim arising from the Thompson TRID issue or the data breach' and that any claim 'would hit during the period Keystone has no E&O coverage.'
✓Run #6Section 4 of the briefing explicitly discusses how the E&O insurance gap and data breach intersect: if breach claims arise while coverage has lapsed, Keystone would face regulatory obligations and lawsuits without professional liability insurance in place.

tool selection

✗Run #2The agent called mortgage_los_list_staff with an empty parameter object ({}) — no active_only parameter was set to false. The results returned 12 active staff members and did NOT include Rachel Kim (terminated), confirming the default is active_only=true. ✗Run #3The agent never called mortgage_los_list_staff or mortgage_los_get_staff. It searched emails and contacts but did not use the Mortgage LOS tool to verify Rachel Kim's employment/termination status or look up her staff record.

There is no call to mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only=false or mortgage_los_get_staff with Rachel Kim's staff ID. The default mortgage_los_list_staff call would only return active staff, not Rachel Kim who was terminated.
✗Run #5The agent called mortgage_los_list_staff with no parameters (default), returning only 12 active staff members with no Rachel Kim. The agent also searched mortgage_los_search_borrowers for Rachel Kim, which is incorrect (she's staff, not a borrower). No call was made with active_only=false or mortgage_los_get_staff with Rachel Kim's staff ID.
✗Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_list_staff with empty input {} (no active_only=false parameter). The result returned 12 active staff with no Rachel Kim present, confirming the default active_only=true was used. The criterion requires active_only=false or a direct lookup of Rachel Kim's staff record. The agent never called this tool with parameters that would return terminated employees.
